title: werauhia jenii
intro: Werauhia jenii, also known as the Māori's gold, is a species of flowering plant in the Aspleniaceae family, native to New Zealand. Traditionally, the plant's rhizomes and roots have been used in Māori medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral activities. In terms of herbal preparations, the rhizomes and roots of Werauhia jenii can be dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
